title: Jenkins Insurance Full Demo Site + Complete Admin Panel

requirements.text: Build the full Jenkins Insurance Agency public-facing demo site by composing all Jenkins-branded section components in JenkinsPublicSite.tsx: sticky header (JenkinsHeader), full-screen hero with animated headline and float effect (JenkinsHero), gold trust bar with count-up stats (JenkinsTrustBar), carrier badges grid (JenkinsCarrierBadges), Why Choose Us cards (JenkinsWhyChoose), coverage types grid (JenkinsCoverageTypes), About C. Jenkins section (JenkinsAbout), Google Reviews section (JenkinsReviews), Quote/Contact form (JenkinsQuoteContact), Spanish bilingual section (JenkinsBilingual), footer with Agent Login link (JenkinsFooter), floating WhatsApp button (JenkinsFloatingWhatsApp), and mobile sticky bottom bar (JenkinsStickyMobileBar). Document title must be set to 'Jenkins Insurance Agency | Greater Houston TX'. All scroll animations use IntersectionObserver. Colors: Navy #1B3A6B, Gold #F4B942, White #FFFFFF. Fonts: Montserrat (headings), Open Sans (body).
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 sections render correctly in sequence on the public page
Sticky nav transitions background on scroll
Hero headline and agent photo animate on load
Trust bar count-up animations trigger on scroll into view
WhatsApp floating button has pulse animation
Mobile sticky bar shows Call Now and WhatsApp buttons
All 6 city names appear in footer service areas
Phone (281) 410-8934 appears in header and contact section

requirements.text: Add an 'Agent Login →' link/button to the JenkinsFooter component that navigates to /admin.

quotes: Access: A button in footer that says 'Agent Login →'

acceptanceCriteria: Footer displays 'Agent Login →' text link
Clicking it navigates to /admin route

requirements.text: Create an /admin route in App.tsx that renders an AdminLoginPage. The login page must display: centered card with navy/gold styling, the text 'Jenkins Insurance Admin Panel' as a title, a password input field, and a 'Login' button. The demo password is 'admin123'. On correct password, store authentication in sessionStorage key 'adminAuthenticated' = 'true' and redirect to /admin/dashboard. On incorrect password, show an inline error message. The login page must not show the sidebar or top bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /admin shows the login page when not authenticated
Correct password (admin123) redirects to /admin/dashboard
Wrong password shows error message
Authentication state persisted in sessionStorage

requirements.text: Create a full admin panel layout component (AdminJenkinsLayout) with: a fixed left sidebar (260px wide, navy #1B3A6B background) containing the menu items with emoji icons — Dashboard, Quote Requests, Reviews Manager, Edit Website Content, Contact Info Settings, Service Areas, Analytics, Settings, Logout; a top bar (white background) showing 'Welcome, C. Jenkins', today's date, a notification bell icon with a red dot badge, and a gold 'View My Website →' button that links to '/'; a main content area (white, scrollable) that renders child routes via Outlet. Active sidebar menu item is highlighted gold (#F4B942 text or background). Sidebar collapses to a hamburger drawer on mobile. Smooth transition animations on sidebar open/close. All /admin/* routes (except /admin itself) are protected — redirect to /admin if not authenticated.

quotes: Top bar (white, with agent name + logout)

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar is 260px wide on desktop, navy background
All 9 menu items visible with emoji icons
Active route menu item is highlighted gold
Top bar shows 'Welcome, C. Jenkins', current date, bell with red dot, 'View My Website →' button
Mobile: sidebar collapses and opens via hamburger
Unauthenticated access to any /admin/* route redirects to /admin

requirements.text: Build the Admin Dashboard page (/admin/dashboard) as the default page after login. Display 4 stat cards in a row: Card 1 (navy): icon 📋, value '12', label 'Quote Requests This Month', subtext '↑ +3 from last month' in green. Card 2 (gold): icon ⭐, value '4.9', label 'Google Rating', subtext '127 total reviews'. Card 3 (green): icon 💰, value '$4,200', label 'Est. Revenue This Month', subtext 'Based on 12 leads × avg policy'. Card 4 (navy): icon 👁️, value '847', label 'Website Visitors This Month', subtext '↑ +23% from last month' in green. Below cards: a 'Recent Quote Requests' table with columns Name, City, Coverage, Date, Status showing 5 sample rows (Sarah M./The Woodlands/Home+Auto/Today/New, Robert T./Humble/Flood/Yesterday/Contacted, Maria G./Spring/Business/Mar 1/Quoted, Jennifer K./Magnolia/Auto/Feb 28/Contacted, David L./Tomball/Life/Feb 27/Closed). Each row has a navy 'View' button and a gold 'Mark as Contacted' button. Status badges are color-coded (yellow=New, green=Contacted, blue=Quoted/Quote Sent, green checkmark=Closed). On the right side, a 'Quick Actions' card with buttons: '+ Add New Review' (gold), 'Edit Phone Number' (navy), 'Update Business Hours' (navy), 'Download All Leads CSV' (navy).
acceptanceCriteria: 4 stat cards render with correct values, colors, and icons
Recent Quote Requests table shows all 5 sample rows
Status badges are correctly color-coded
Each row has View (navy) and Mark as Contacted (gold) buttons
Quick Actions card shows all 4 buttons
Dashboard is the default page after login

requirements.text: Build the Quote Requests page (/admin/quotes). Display a filter bar with: search input (by name/email), City dropdown (6 cities), Coverage Type dropdown, Status dropdown, date range inputs, and a gold 'Export to CSV' button that downloads lead data as CSV. Display a sortable table with columns: #, Name, Phone, Email, City, Coverage, Message, Date, Status, Actions. Status badges: yellow=New, green=Contacted, blue=Quote Sent, green checkmark=Closed, red X=Not Interested. Actions per row: View (opens modal), Change Status (inline dropdown), Add Notes, Delete (with confirmation dialog). Pre-populate with at least 8 sample leads including the 5 from the dashboard. Clicking 'View' opens a modal popup showing full lead details, an editable Notes textarea, a Status dropdown, a green 'Send WhatsApp' button (opens wa.me link), a navy 'Send Email' button (opens mailto), and a gold 'Mark Closed — $749 Won!' button. All lead data stored and retrieved from localStorage. Sorting by any column header click. Delete requires a confirmation dialog before removing.

quotes: Confirmation dialog before any delete action

acceptanceCriteria: Filter bar renders all 5 filter controls plus Export CSV button
CSV export downloads leads data as a .csv file
Table shows all columns and is sortable by clicking headers
Status badges are color-coded correctly
Delete shows confirmation dialog before removing
View modal shows all fields including editable notes, status dropdown, WhatsApp, Email, and Mark Closed buttons
Lead data persists in localStorage

requirements.text: Build the Reviews Manager page (/admin/reviews). Display current reviews as cards, each showing star rating (filled stars), review text, reviewer name, city, and date. Each card has edit (✏️), delete (🗑️ with confirmation), move up (⬆️), and move down (⬇️) buttons. A gold '+ Add New Review' button at the top opens a form (inline or modal) with: star rating selector (clickable 1–5 stars), Reviewer Name input, Reviewer City dropdown (6 cities), Review Text textarea, Date input, and 'Save Review' gold button. An 'Overall Rating' display auto-calculates the average of all reviews and updates live. Pre-populate with at least 3 sample reviews. All review data stored in localStorage. Changes to reviews (add/edit/delete/reorder) are reflected immediately when the public site reads from localStorage.

quotes: Overall rating: Auto-calculates average from all reviews
Updates live on main website instantly

acceptanceCriteria: Reviews render as cards with all required fields
Add New Review form opens with all fields including star selector
Edit and Delete work with confirmation on delete
Move Up/Down reorders reviews
Overall rating auto-calculates and updates when reviews change
Data persists in localStorage

requirements.text: Build the Edit Website Content page (/admin/content). Display expandable accordion/card sections: HERO SECTION (main headline, subheadline, button 1 text, button 2 text, 4 trust indicator fields), ABOUT SECTION (agent name, about text textarea, years experience, families served number, carriers count), CONTACT INFO (phone number, email address, physical address, Mon-Fri hours, Saturday hours, WhatsApp number), COVERAGE TYPES (title + description for each of 6 coverage cards), BILINGUAL SECTION (Spanish headline, Spanish body text, Spanish button text). Each section has a gold 'Save Changes' button and a navy 'Preview Changes' button. All changes saved to localStorage with keys matching the corresponding section. The public site's Jenkins components must read from localStorage (falling back to default values) so changes appear immediately on the live site after saving. Show a success toast '✅ Saved successfully!' bottom-right after any save.

quotes: Changes show on live website immediately after saving
Success toast notifications: '✅ Saved successfully!' appears bottom right after any save

acceptanceCriteria: All 5 expandable sections render with their respective fields
Each section has Save Changes (gold) and Preview Changes (navy) buttons
Saving stores data to localStorage
Public site Jenkins components read from localStorage and reflect changes
Success toast appears bottom-right after saving
Default values shown when localStorage is empty

requirements.text: Build the Analytics page (/admin/analytics). Top row: 4 stat cards for Total Visitors This Month, Total Quote Form Submissions, WhatsApp Button Clicks, Phone Number Clicks (use sample numbers). Bar chart 'Monthly Quote Requests' showing 6 months: Oct=4, Nov=6, Dec=5, Jan=8, Feb=10, Mar=12 — gold (#F4B942) bars, white card background, border-radius 16px, padding 32px — implemented with Recharts BarChart or CSS bars. Pie/donut chart 'Leads by Coverage Type': Homeowners 35%, Auto 28%, Bundle 20%, Business 10%, Other 7% — implemented with Recharts PieChart. Horizontal bar chart 'Leads by City': The Woodlands 32%, Spring 24%, Humble 20%, Magnolia 12%, Tomball 8%, Conroe 4% — rendered as CSS progress bars with gold fill.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 top stat cards render with sample data
Monthly Quote Requests bar chart shows correct 6-month data with gold bars
Leads by Coverage Type pie/donut chart shows 5 segments with correct percentages
Leads by City shows horizontal bars with correct city names and percentages
All charts are responsive

requirements.text: Build the Settings page (/admin/settings) with 4 sections: PROFILE (Agent Full Name input, License Number input labeled 'TX Dept of Insurance', Agency Name input, Profile Photo upload button); NOTIFICATIONS (4 toggle switches — gold when on — for: 'Email me when new quote arrives', 'WhatsApp alert for new leads', 'Weekly summary email', 'Monthly analytics report', all defaulting to on); WEBSITE (Custom domain text field with placeholder 'yourname.com', 'Connect Domain' button, Google Maps embed URL field, Facebook Page URL field, LinkedIn Profile URL field); CHANGE PASSWORD (Current password, New password, Confirm password inputs, 'Update Password' navy button). Each section has its own save button. Notification toggles and other settings persist to localStorage. Show success toast on save.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 settings sections render with correct fields
Notification toggles are gold when on, defaulting to all enabled
Toggle states persist to localStorage
Update Password validates that new password and confirm password match
Success toast appears after saving each section

requirements.text: Build a Contact Info Settings page (/admin/contact) and a Service Areas page (/admin/service-areas) as stubs that are accessible from the sidebar. Contact Info Settings shows editable fields for phone, email, address, and business hours (same as the Contact Info sub-section in Edit Website Content), saved to localStorage. Service Areas shows a list of the 6 cities (The Woodlands, Spring, Humble, Magnolia, Tomball, Conroe) with checkboxes to enable/disable each, plus an 'Add City' input, saved to localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact Info Settings page renders with phone, email, address, hours fields
Service Areas page shows 6 cities with checkboxes
Both pages are accessible from sidebar navigation
Changes persist to localStorage

requirements.text: Implement a global toast notification system in the admin panel. A success toast with '✅ Saved successfully!' text appears at the bottom-right of the screen for 3 seconds after any save action across all admin pages. Implement as a shared context or component used throughout the admin layout.

quotes: Success toast notifications: '✅ Saved successfully!' appears bottom right after any save

acceptanceCriteria: Toast appears bottom-right after any save action
Toast auto-dismisses after ~3 seconds
Toast has a green/navy style consistent with brand
All save buttons show a loading spinner while saving

requirements.text: Update all Jenkins public site components (JenkinsHero, JenkinsAbout, JenkinsBilingual, JenkinsCoverageTypes, JenkinsReviews, JenkinsQuoteContact, JenkinsFooter) to read their content from localStorage (using the same keys written by the Edit Website Content admin page) and fall back to hardcoded default values when no localStorage data exists. This enables live content editing from the admin panel to appear on the public site immediately.

quotes: Changes to content reflect on main site immediately
All data stored in browser localStorage for demo purposes

acceptanceCriteria: JenkinsHero reads headline, subheadline, button text from localStorage
JenkinsAbout reads agent name, bio, stats from localStorage
JenkinsBilingual reads Spanish text fields from localStorage
JenkinsCoverageTypes reads card titles/descriptions from localStorage
JenkinsReviews reads review list from localStorage
All components fall back to hardcoded defaults when localStorage keys are absent

requirements.text: Register all admin routes in App.tsx: /admin (login page, public), /admin/dashboard, /admin/quotes, /admin/reviews, /admin/content, /admin/contact, /admin/service-areas, /admin/analytics, /admin/settings. All routes except /admin are wrapped in the AdminJenkinsLayout and protected by sessionStorage authentication check. The /admin/dashboard route is the default landing after login.

quotes: Dashboard loads as default after login

acceptanceCriteria: All 9 admin routes are registered and accessible
Unauthenticated access to protected routes redirects to /admin login
After login, user lands on /admin/dashboard
Logout clears sessionStorage and redirects to /admin login

requirements.text: Update the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo) to add a `submitQuote` public function that accepts a quote submission record (name, phone, email, city, coverageType, message, timestamp) and stores it in a stable array. Add a `getQuotes` admin-accessible function that returns all stored quote submissions. This enables real quote form submissions from the public site to be stored on-chain, while the admin panel can also display localStorage-based demo leads alongside backend leads.
acceptanceCriteria: submitQuote function accepts and stores quote records
getQuotes returns all stored quotes
Quote form submission on public site calls the backend function
Backend compiles without errors

constraints: Colors must strictly follow: Navy #1B3A6B, Gold #F4B942, White #FFFFFF
Admin authentication uses sessionStorage key 'adminAuthenticated' with demo password 'admin123'
All admin panel data (leads, reviews, content) stored in localStorage for demo purposes
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Jenkins public site components must fall back to hardcoded defaults when localStorage is empty
Phone number (281) 410-8934 and agent C. Jenkins must appear throughout
nonGoals: Real email or WhatsApp API integration (use mailto: and wa.me: links only)
Multi-user authentication or role-based access control
Server-side content storage for the admin panel (localStorage only for demo)
Internet Identity authentication for the admin panel

required: Professional headshot-style portrait of a confident male insurance agent in a navy blue suit with gold tie, neutral studio background, warm lighting, business professional (agent-photo-jenkins.dim_400x500.png)
Jenkins Insurance Agency logo mark — bold serif or sans-serif text 'Jenkins Insurance' with a shield icon in navy and gold on white background (jenkins-logo.dim_300x80.png)
Admin panel dashboard preview screenshot mockup showing navy sidebar and gold stat cards on white background (admin-preview-dashboard.dim_800x500.png)
